seoTitle: Glenugie vs Glenwood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Glenugie and Glenwood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Glenugie and Glenwood.
heading: Glenugie vs Glenwood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Glenugie, New South Wales and Glenwood, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Glenwood has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Glenugie (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Glenugie or Glenwood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Glenugie has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Glenwood scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Glenugie scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Glenwood scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Glenugie or Glenwood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Glenwood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
